extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE Julian won by attacking on the final 1.3km Mur de Huy ascent, holding off FUGLSANG Jakob's late surge by staying left to close the door
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE punctured before key climbs but Deceuninck had spare disc-brake bike ready, showing equipment protocol matters for protected riders
Lotto-Soudal attacked repeatedly mid-race with WELLENS presumably to exhaust rivals before final Mur, showing pre-emptive attrition tactics
FUGLSANG launched earlier on final 1.3km Mur creating small gap, forcing ALAPHILIPPE to bridge then counter rather than pure sprint
ALAPHILIPPE used left side of road to block FUGLSANG's second acceleration attempt, tactical positioning matters on narrow finish

rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: positive: multiple second places in major races signals consistent elite form but possible mental/tactical weakness in closers
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: finished outside top-10 despite positioning advantage, suggests declining explosive power on steep finale
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: team attacked repeatedly for him mid-race but ultimately not present in finale, attrition tactics failed
rider_signals.ULISSI Diego: positive: podium third after DAN MARTIN dropped early, showing UAE had depth when leader failed
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: top-10 finish on steep punchy climb reveals underestimated climbing development for pure sprinter type
